CIPHER.275.cipherString: But my experience is that people who have been through painful, difficult times are filled with compassion.
CIPHER.275.question: Someone taped a piece of paper with this sequence onto the back of a trophy from last year's Science Olympiad. It's a quote by Amy Grant, written using an Aristocrat Cipher. What does it say?
CIPHER.275.author: Amy Grant

CIPHER.252.question: Someone left this encrypted quote scribbled on the chalkboard before class began. It's a quote, written as a Cryptarithm. What do the values 98 73458 23106 decode to?

CIPHER.263.cipherString: There was beauty in the idea of freedom, but it was an illusion. Every human heart was chained by love.
CIPHER.263.question: This coded note is discovered inside a sealed envelope labeled “Confidential.” It's a quote by Cassandra Clare, encoded using an Aristocrat Cipher. What does it say?
CIPHER.263.author: Cassandra Clare

CIPHER.260.cipherString: We cannot live in this reality. If we want to save the future, then we have to repair the past.
CIPHER.260.question: Scratched onto the lens of an abandoned telescope, decode this Complete Columnar quote by Jean-Luc Picard. The quote has SAVE THE somewhere in it. What does it say?
CIPHER.260.author: Jean-Luc Picard

CIPHER.253.cipherString: Art is the imposing of a pattern on experience, and our aesthetic enjoyment in recognition of the pattern.
CIPHER.253.question: Your lava lamp started bubbling in Aristocrat code by Alfred North Whitehead using a K2 alphabet. What does it say?
CIPHER.253.author: Alfred North Whitehead

CIPHER.249.cipherString: Now that the pass has gone
CIPHER.249.question: Your robot vacuum just projected this message onto the ceiling. It's a quote by Sindiwe Magona encoded with a Hill with a keyword of PLOD. What does it say?
CIPHER.249.author: Sindiwe Magona

CIPHER.271.cipherString: It was one of those perfect English autumnal days which occur more frequently in memory than in life.
CIPHER.271.question: Someone passed you a piece of paper with this Aristocrat encoded quote by P.D. James. What does it say?
CIPHER.271.author: P.D. James

CIPHER.268.cipherString: Problems can become opportunities when the right people come together
CIPHER.268.question: You receive this cryptic message from the future through an online time capsule. It's a quote by Robert South, written in a Complete Columnar. The quote has RIGHT somewhere in it. What does it say?
CIPHER.268.author: Robert South

CIPHER.257.cipherString: We have outlined under a number of headings our objectives and the ideal for which we struggle.
CIPHER.257.question: While scrolling through an old floppy disk labeled "TOP SECRET", you discovered this quote by Errico Malatesta hidden as an Aristocrat using a K2 alphabet. You are told that the cipher contains the word our. What does it say?
CIPHER.257.author: Errico Malatesta

CIPHER.266.cipherString: A clever person solves a problem. A wise person avoids it.
CIPHER.266.question: You discover a scrap of paper tucked inside an old lab notebook. It contains a quote by Albert Einstein encoded as a Nihilist Substitution with a keyword of MEDALS and Polybius key of UNIVERSAL. What does it say?
CIPHER.266.author: Albert Einstein

CIPHER.259.cipherString: Esta cosa... que puede ser entendido y no expresado, puede tomar un género neutro, y todo escolar lo sabe.
CIPHER.259.question: An explorer's journal mentions a “hidden truth” encoded below. It turns out to be a quote by Jeremy Taylor in Spanish, written in a Xenocrypt Cipher using a K1 alphabet. What does it say?
CIPHER.259.author: Jeremy Taylor
CIPHER.259.translation: This thing...that can be understood and not expressed, may take a neuter gender;--and every schoolboy knows it.

CIPHER.256.cipherString: This day says nothing different to me than to you.
CIPHER.256.question: Scrawled in dry-erase marker on the whiteboard after hours: this Baconian encoded quote by Neamat Imam. What does it say?
CIPHER.256.author: Neamat Imam

CIPHER.265.cipherString: Competition is great. And as long as it's friendly and not a malicious thing, then I think it's cool.
CIPHER.265.question: A riddle is discovered taped under a chemistry lab bench. It's a quote by Janet Jackson, encrypted as an Aristocrat. You are told that the cipher contains the word AS twice.  What does it say?
CIPHER.265.author: Janet Jackson

CIPHER.274.cipherString: I don't care anything about reasons, but I know what I like.
CIPHER.274.question: Your science fair project logs contain a mysterious string of letters. It's a quote by Henry James, encoded as a Fractionated Morse. You are told that the quote starts with IDONT. What does it say?
CIPHER.274.author: Henry James

CIPHER.269.cipherString: Throw yourself into each moment as if it were the only one that really existed. Work and work hard.
CIPHER.269.question: A mysterious QR code on the back of a cereal box leads to this encrypted quote by Jacques Lusseyran using the Aristocrat Cipher using a K1 alphabet. What does it say?
CIPHER.269.author: Jacques Lusseyran

CIPHER.261.cipherString: You are judged many times more by what you do in groups than for what you do as individuals.
CIPHER.261.question: A message was left on the whiteboard after science club. It's a quote by Compton Gage encoded using a Checkerboard with a polybius key of COMPLAINER. What does it say?
CIPHER.261.author: Compton Gage

CIPHER.273.cipherString: I don't know of any sport where the goalposts can shift the way they do with endurance swimming.
CIPHER.273.question: You open a forgotten email draft saved in the school's computer system. It holds a quote by Lewis Pugh encoded in an Aristocrat using a K1 alphabet. What was the K1 Keyword used to encode it?
CIPHER.273.author: Lewis Pugh

CIPHER.272.cipherString: If bread is the first necessity of life, recreation is a close second.
CIPHER.272.question: An old robot prototype suddenly prints this encoded text. It's a quote by Edward Bellamy, encrypted using a Porta using a keyword of PARODY. What does it say?
CIPHER.272.author: Edward Bellamy

CIPHER.262.cipherString: Have as little suspicion as possible and conceal that.
CIPHER.262.question: Found inside a dissected frog's stomach (plastic, don’t worry): this Baconian note by John Lancaster Spalding. What does it say?
CIPHER.262.author: John Lancaster Spalding

CIPHER.264.question: Found taped under a desk in study hall: this Cryptarithm puzzle. What do the values 1540 01089 76532 decode to?

CIPHER.250.cipherString: a person who tries to use reasoning to explain faith gets lost in the wilderness of incomprehension.
CIPHER.250.question: You found the following carved into the bark of a hollow log. You are told that it's a quote by Janvier Chando encoded as an Aristocrat. You are told that the cipher contains the word TO twice. What does it say?
CIPHER.250.author: Janvier Chando

CIPHER.270.cipherString: You have to hold back yourself. Calm down; your heart will prompt you to achieve nothing.
CIPHER.270.question: This coded passage was discovered in the margin of a physics exam from years ago. It's a quote, encrypted as a Checkerboard with a Polybius key of BEHAVIOURS. What does it say?

CIPHER.251.cipherString: One to another cannot be a perfect physician.
CIPHER.251.question: You find this encoded line written in invisible ink on a lab safety poster. It's a quote by George Herbert, written in a Baconian. You are told that the deciphered text starts with ONET. What does it say?
CIPHER.251.author: George Herbert

CIPHER.258.cipherString: Work expands so as to fill the time available for its completion.
CIPHER.258.question: Archaeologists unearth a stone tablet inscribed with a message. It's a quote by C. Northcote Parkinson encoded as a Complete Columnar. The quote has TIME somewhere in it. What does it say?
CIPHER.258.author: C. Northcote Parkinson

CIPHER.255.cipherString: Tennis is a perfect combination of violent action taking place in an atmosphere of total tranquillity.
CIPHER.255.question: The gym's scoreboard glitched during dodgeball to show this Aristocrat Cipher by Billie Jean King using a K1 alphabet. You are told that the cipher contains OF. What does it say?
CIPHER.255.author: Billie Jean King

CIPHER.254.cipherString: Do not counsel what is most pleasant, but what is best.
CIPHER.254.question: Upon searching a room, the following were found on scraps of paper. You are told it's a quote by Solon encoded as a Nihilist Substitution with a keyword of ALMOST and polybius key of PARCHMENT. What does it say?
CIPHER.254.author: Solon

CIPHER.267.cipherString: Wit is the sudden marriage of ideas which before their union were not perceived to have any relation.
CIPHER.267.question: A coded message appears after you shine a UV light on a classroom poster. It turns out to be a quote by Mark Twain, encrypted using an Aristocrat using a K2 alphabet. What was the K2 Keyword used to encode it?
CIPHER.267.author: Mark Twain
